steps.title: Use two implied table points
steps.content: This is an exponential model question, so a fast Desmos method is to make a tiny table. The problem gives $q(0)=14$. A $45\%$ decrease means the next value keeps $55\%$ of the previous value, so $q(1)=0.55(14)=7.7$.

steps.title: Run exponential regression
steps.content: Type the regression row $y_1\sim a b^{x_1}$. Desmos returns $a=14$ and $b=0.55$, so the function is $q(x)=14(0.55)^x$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice C is the only option with initial value $14$ and decay factor $0.55$. Choice A swaps the coefficient and base, choices B and D use growth factors, and choice C is correct.
